model_narrative: We arrange a collection of colored items into an ordered list of positions, one item per position. Each color comes with a fixed number of items that all have to be placed. We want items of the same color to be spread out as evenly as possible rather than bunched together, and we model the ordering of each color as a flow through a small routing network that visits that color's chosen positions in order. The objective is to minimize the total spacing penalty, which measures how far the gaps between consecutive same-color items stray from perfectly even spacing across the whole schedule.

sets.name: CACT
sets.doc: the colors that actually have one or more items to place; colors with a count of zero are dropped and never appear here

sets.name: S
sets.doc: the ordered positions on the schedule, one per item to be placed, numbered consecutively from the first position onward

sets.name: Arcs
sets.doc: the directed arcs of a per-color routing network whose nodes are a special source node labeled 0 together with the schedule positions; an arc carries flow of a single color from one node to a later node, and there are also return arcs from each position back to the source node 0; for a given color, an item placed at the from-node is followed next by an item of that color at the to-node, so traversing an arc moves from one occurrence of a color to its next occurrence

params.name: nc
params.doc: the number of items of each color that must be placed somewhere on the schedule, as a whole number

params.name: dev
params.doc: the spacing penalty charged for routing a color along an arc, equal to how far the gap between the two endpoints of the arc departs from that color's ideal even spacing; arcs touching the source node 0 carry no penalty

vars.name: f
vars.doc: the amount of flow sent along each arc for its color; one unit of flow traces the ordered sequence of positions used by a color

vars.name: p
vars.doc: one when the given color is assigned to the given position, zero otherwise

vars.name: obj
vars.doc: the total spacing penalty accumulated over the whole schedule

constraints.name: f[c,
constraints.intent: For each color at each position, the routing of that color has to be conserved. The amount of that color's flow arriving at the position must equal the amount leaving it, so nothing accumulates or disappears at any position.

constraints.name: f[c,
constraints.intent: Every color that has items to place starts its routing exactly once. For each such color, the total flow leaving the source node and entering the schedule must be exactly one unit.

constraints.name: f[c,
constraints.intent: A position is used by a color exactly when that color's routing passes through it. For each color at each position, the total flow of that color leaving the position must equal whether that color is assigned to that position.

constraints.name: p[c,
constraints.intent: Each color must end up placed the required number of times. For each color, the number of positions assigned to it across the whole schedule must equal that color's required item count.

constraints.name: p[c,
constraints.intent: Every position holds exactly one item. For each position, exactly one color may be assigned to it.

constraints.name: obj
constraints.intent: The total spacing penalty accounts for every arc the routings traverse. Set the total penalty equal to the sum over all arcs of that arc's spacing penalty weighted by the amount of flow sent along it.

query: There's a proposal on the table to give the green spots more presence up front: across the opening ten slots, we'd want green placements to outnumber white placements by at least one. Work that in and tell me where the spacing score lands.
expected_pyomo: model.greens_lead_whites = Constraint(expr=sum(model.p['G', str(i)] for i in range(1, 11)) >= sum(model.p['W', str(i)] for i in range(1, 11)) + 1)

query: We're evaluating opening the reel with two blue spots back-to-back in the first two slots. Plug that in — what's the resulting deviation?

query: Why fan all three white spots across the whole reel? Couldn't we just park them together at the tail end, slots 18 through 20, and keep things tidy? What's the cost the model is seeing there?
expected_pyomo: model.want_whites_tail = Constraint(expr=sum(model.p['W', str(i)] for i in range(18, 21)) == 3)
